Udoka publicly criticized the team's execution and composure.

The series deficit now stands at 0‑3 for Houston.

articles.title: LeBron's clutch 3 lifts Lakers to OT win and 3‑0 lead
articles.original_title: Lakers' last-minute magic keys OT win for 3-0 lead
articles.summary: LeBron James scored 29 points, including a tying three‑pointer with 13 seconds left in regulation, to force overtime against the Rockets. Los Angeles capitalized on Houston’s missing Kevin Durant and won 112‑108, taking a 3‑0 series advantage in the first round.
key_points: LeBron finished with 29 points and hit the decisive 3‑pointer late in the game.
The Rockets were without Kevin Durant, weakening their defense.
Lakers secured a 112‑108 overtime victory, moving up 3‑0 in the series.

articles.title: Harper sparks Spurs comeback, Castle leads with 33
articles.original_title: Harper stars off bench to rally Wemby-less Spurs
articles.summary: Rookie Dylan Harper contributed 27 points off the bench while Stephon Castle posted a game‑high 33 as the San Antonio Spurs rallied past the Portland Trail Blazers. The win gave San Antonio a 2‑1 lead in the series despite missing Victor Wembanyama.
key_points: Harper provided 27 points as a reserve.
Castle’s 33 points were the highest of the game.
Spurs took a 2‑1 series edge over the Blazers without Wembanyama.

articles.title: Quickley out for Raptors’ first‑round series after hamstring setback
articles.original_title: Raptors' Quickley aggravates injury, out for series
articles.summary: Toronto Raptors guard Immanuel Quickley aggravated a strained right hamstring while attempting a comeback from the injury and will miss the upcoming first‑round playoff series against the Cleveland Cavaliers. The team confirmed his unavailability on Friday.
key_points: Quickley’s hamstring strain worsened during rehab activities.
He will be sidelined for the entire first‑round series versus Cleveland.
Raptors lose a key rotational guard heading into the playoffs.
